name: Panagiotis Kaimasidis
headline: Mechanical & Aeronautical Engineer (MSc) · Process & Operations Specialist
cv_text:
PANAGIOTIS KAIMASIDIS
Mechanical & Aeronautical Engineer (MSc) • Process & Operations Specialist
Athens, Greece | panagiotiskaimasidis@gmail.com | linkedin.com/in/PKaimasidis

ENGINEERING & FMCG MANUFACTURING EXPERIENCE

PROCTER & GAMBLE (P&G) — Project Management & Engineering Intern | Brussels, Belgium | Aug 2024 – Dec 2024
• Process Optimization: Redesigned chemical storage and fluid workflows, eliminating operational bottlenecks and expanding dosing capacity by 400% through high-efficiency modular infrastructure.
• Data-Driven Controls: Integrated inline flowmeter systems and automated sensor feedback loops to optimize process parameters, maximize dosing accuracy, and significantly reduce process upsets.
• Reliability & Availability: Analyzed, classified, and catalogued critical engineering spare parts while qualifying alternative technical vendors, mitigating supply risks and reducing potential equipment downtime.
• Digital Transformation: Architected and deployed advanced computer vision quality control (QC) systems on production lines, cutting manual inspection cycles, minimizing waste, and boosting automated error detection.

NEW MUNICIPALITY COURT OF PIRAEUS — Health & Safety Engineer (Freelance) | Piraeus, Greece | Dec 2025 – Present
• Operations Leadership: Directing end-to-end health, safety, and environmental operations for the first LEED-certified public facility in Greece, governing safety standards for a daily site workforce of over 400 personnel.
• Standardized Compliance: Authoring and executing rigorous site-wide safety protocols, hazard assessments, and operational monitoring metrics to achieve strict alignment with green building standards and national regulatory compliance.

HELLENIC AIRFORCE — Aeronautical Engineer Intern | Andravida Air Base, Greece | July 2023 – Aug 2023
• Troubleshooting & RCA: Executed complex 2nd-degree maintenance actions and systematic Root Cause Analysis (RCA) on F-4 Phantom (J-79) turbojet engines to restore baseline operational and asset conditions.
• Run to Standard: Conducted comprehensive ground testing and real-time performance logging of overhauled propulsion units to validate technical parameters and guarantee flight-readiness to absolute military standards.

LEADERSHIP & BUSINESS EXPERIENCE

EUROAVIA — Business Relations Coordinator & Treasurer | Patras, Greece | 2020 – Present
• Sponsorship & Negotiation: Formulated and executed corporate contract negotiations, successfully securing over €60,000 in industrial backing from major engineering and commercial partners.
• Financial Governance: Managed comprehensive budget allocations, treasury books, and strict operational expenditure controls for the local branch to optimize cash flow and resource distribution (2021–2022).

EDUCATION

UNIVERSITY OF PATRAS — MSc in Mechanical & Aeronautical Engineering | Patras, Greece | Graduated: Oct 2025
• Academic Standout: Graduated with an excellent overall score of 8.36 / 10.
• Master's Thesis: Wire DED Additive Manufacturing Process Model for Part Quality Optimization — Built advanced physics-based numerical simulations (C) to forecast thermal dynamics, mitigate material defects, and optimize process execution parameters.

TECHNICAL SKILLS & LANGUAGES
Core Engineering: Process Optimization, Loss & Waste Reduction, Root Cause Analysis (RCA), QA Automation, Supply Chain Resilience, 5S Standards, Run to Standard (R2S), Asset Reliability.
Software Tools: Siemens Teamcenter (PLM), CATIA V5, AutoCAD, Unity (AR), MATLAB, Python, C, C#.
Languages: English (Proficient / Michigan Proficiency), French (Competent / DELF B2), Greek (Native).
career_vision: Panagiotis wants to solve genuinely hard, high-impact engineering and operations problems at a global scale — not tied to a single factory or location. He is open to any industry as long as it is fast-moving, ambitious, and puts him in rooms with brilliant, driven people who constantly challenge him. His ideal trajectory runs from hands-on technical and process excellence toward roles with increasing commercial and strategic weight — influencing major decisions, navigating tough negotiations between parties, and shaping how complex industrial systems work at scale. He thrives under pressure and wants to feel intellectually stretched at all times; the worst outcome is being the smartest person in the room. Global mobility is non-negotiable: the ability to move, travel, and operate across markets is core to his identity. Target companies are large multinationals with global operations (P&G, Shell, Airbus, Siemens, ABB, LVMH, McKinsey, BCG, or similar elite employers) or high-growth technology and industrial companies where deep engineering expertise intersects with real business impact. Seniority: early-career (MSc graduated Oct 2025, 0–2 years experience). Avoid: pure factory-floor production roles with no strategic dimension, niche SMEs with limited growth trajectory, roles anchored to a single city long-term, and anything purely administrative or sales-focused.
